comment: Default-FAIL verification contract. Checks flipped to true only after their command ran and evidence was inspected this session. Stack is shell (uninstall.sh / uninstall.ps1); same waiver precedent as T-009's contract (specs/local-env-mcp/verification/T-009.contract.json) applies to lint/typecheck/build. unit-tests and acceptance-tests cite uninstall-tests.log (24 passed / 0 failed) and the T-010 red/green pair, captured the same way as T-009 (uninstall.sh/uninstall.ps1 reverted to pre-ci-mcp committed content while the new 3-MCP test scenarios were kept in place; disclosed deviation in T-010-red.txt). No new removal logic was required for T-010 -- uninstall.sh/uninstall.ps1 already implement a name-list-driven generic removal path (established for local-env-mcp); adding ci-mcp to VALID_MCPS/MCP_LIST/ValidateSet/default array was sufficient, and the existing managed-name-only deletion boundary (scenario (o)/(q)) and broken-JSON failsafe were re-verified to also cover ci-mcp.
